text: IRD Sri Lanka Income Tax page. The page states that income tax is payable for each year of assessment by every individual, company, or body of persons on taxable income, and it is charged under the provisions of the Inland Revenue Act No. 24 of 2017, effective from April 1, 2018. The page notes amendments by the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Act No. 10 of 2021, effective from April 1, 2021, and the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Act No. 2 of 2025, effective from April 1, 2025.

subsection: Sources of income and taxable income
text: The page identifies the main sources of income as service, business, investment, and other sources. Taxable income is calculated as assessable income less qualifying payments and reliefs. The page also states that gains from the realization of investment assets are included in taxable income but are not eligible for the personal relief mentioned on the page. Emoluments from secondary employment that have been subjected to withholding tax are excluded from taxable income.

subsection: Residence basis and personal relief from 2025-04-01
text: A resident individual or resident company is liable to income tax on income from employment, business, investment, or other sources wherever the source arises. A non-resident individual or non-resident company is liable to income tax on income from employment, business, investment, or other sources to the extent that the income arises in or is derived from Sri Lanka. With effect from April 1, 2025, the aggregate relief including personal relief for every resident individual is Rs. 1,800,000. This relief is not available for gains from the realization of investment assets.

For the period from April 1, 2023 to March 31, 2025, the aggregate relief including personal relief for every resident individual was Rs. 1,200,000. This historical relief was not available for gains from the realization of investment assets.

For the period from April 1, 2020 to March 31, 2023, the aggregate relief including personal relief for every resident individual was Rs. 3,000,000. This historical relief was not available for gains from the realization of investment assets.

subsection: Acts and related resources listed on the page
text: The Income Tax page lists downloadable or linked references including the Inland Revenue Act No. 24 of 2017, the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Act No. 10 of 2021, the Inland Revenue (Amendment) Act No. 2 of 2025, income tax rates, withholding tax rates, a Guide to PAYE Tax for 2018/2019 and 2019/2020, PAYE tax tables 2018/2019 and 2019/2020, and tables for taxes to be deducted under section 83A. It also links to guidance for the Statement of Estimated Tax payable for installment payers.

The page says that every installment payer should pay income tax in installments and that the amount payable for each installment should be calculated by the taxpayer. Due dates listed are: first installment on or before August 15 of that year of assessment; second installment on or before November 15 of that year of assessment; third installment on or before February 15 of that year of assessment; fourth installment on or before May 15 immediately following the end of that year of assessment; and the final installment on or before the date after six months from the end of that year of assessment.

subsection: Statement of Estimated Tax and annual return deadlines
text: The page states that every person should submit a Statement of Estimated Tax payable on or before August 15 in that year of assessment. Every person required to file an income tax return for a year of assessment should submit the return on or before November 30 of the immediately following year of assessment. Returns and the Statement of Estimated Tax should be submitted online through e-Services. The page also says the tax return forms can be downloaded from that page.
